root_text.an4.9:1.1: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, taṇhuppādā yattha bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati.
root_text.an4.9:1.3: Cīvarahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.an4.9:1.4: piṇḍapātahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.an4.9:1.5: senāsanahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati;
root_text.an4.9:1.6: itibhavābhavahetu vā, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjati.
root_text.an4.9:1.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro taṇhuppādā yattha bhikkhuno taṇhā uppajjamānā uppajjatīti.

translation_text.an4.9:1.1: “Mendicants, there are four things that give rise to craving in a mendicant.
translation_text.an4.9:1.6: or rebirth in this or that state.
translation_text.an4.9:1.7: These are the four things that give rise to craving in a mendicant.
translation_text.an4.9:2.2: as they transmigrate on this long journey.
translation_text.an4.9:2.3: They go from this state to another,
translation_text.an4.9:3.2: that craving is the cause of suffering—
translation_text.an4.9:3.3: rid of craving, free of grasping,
